update: Mom robin continues faithfully incubating the three beautiful blue eggs.
funfact: Robins typically lay eggs two or three times a year (and sometimes up to four times). with each nesting cycle producing a clutch of three to five bright blue eggs. The breading season runs from March to August. Because only about 40% of robin nests are sucessful, and individual broods have high predation rates, pairs continuously build fresh, new nests to attempt multiple clutches per season.
